domain: Knowledge Graph Integration for SLM Reasoning
scoring_methodology: opportunity = demand(0-10) x competition(0-10, where 10=no competition) x feasibility(0-10) / 100

market_context.kg_enterprise_trend: GraphRAG architectures rising in 2026; enterprises combining neural + symbolic

ranked_gaps.title: KG-SLM Integration for Agentic Workflows
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: Agentic AI is the dominant trend in 2026. HN threads, Awesome-GraphMemory repo, and papers (Mem0, MAGMA, Graphiti) all confirm massive demand. KG-backed agent memory is proven valuable but no SLM-optimized version exists.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: Graphiti and Cognee serve large LLMs only. Mem0 does not use KGs natively. No tool combines persistent KG memory with SLM-powered agents. The niche is wide open.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Can build on existing agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI) as plugins. KG memory layer is well-understood. SLM inference via Ollama/llama.cpp is mature. Integration glue is the main work.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An open-source SLM-native agent memory library that stores and retrieves structured knowledge via a lightweight KG, with adapters for LangGraph, CrewAI, and AutoGen. Optimized for models under 7B with compact graph serialization and efficient subgraph retrieval for agent context windows.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: AI engineers building cost-efficient autonomous agents, startups that cannot afford cloud LLM API costs for multi-step agent workflows, edge robotics teams needing persistent memory.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Agent frameworks exploding in 2026. SLMs proven 10-30x cheaper. KG memory is the missing piece for reliable, grounded agent behavior without hallucination. First mover gets ecosystem adoption.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium — 3-4 months for MVP. Requires KG storage layer, SLM-aware retrieval, and framework adapters.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $200-500M segment within the $9.4B SLM market, driven by agent infrastructure spend

ranked_gaps.title: Quantized KG-Augmented SLM Inference Engine
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: Highest practitioner demand signal. Users spending $45 testing GraphRAG, community forks for Ollama support, 36-hour indexing times with local models. Real, painful, and widespread.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: nano-GraphRAG supports local models loosely. graphrag-local-ollama is a community hack, not a product. No tool natively combines GGUF/GPTQ quantized inference with KG retrieval in one optimized pipeline. MiniRAG is closest but lacks native quantization integration.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: llama.cpp, Ollama, and vLLM provide mature quantized inference. Graph retrieval is well-understood. The challenge is co-optimizing KG retrieval with quantized model inference for latency.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A unified inference engine that natively loads quantized SLMs (GGUF/AWQ) and co-locates a compact graph index, optimizing the retrieval-generation loop end-to-end. Single binary deployment with built-in KG indexing from documents.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Local AI enthusiasts (r/LocalLLaMA), privacy-conscious enterprises, developers in regions with expensive cloud compute, air-gapped deployments.
ranked_gaps.why_now: GGUF ecosystem is mature (2026). SLM quality at 3-8B is now competitive. The missing piece is native KG integration without the cloud LLM tax.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium-High — 4-6 months. Requires C/C++ or Rust for performance, deep integration with quantization formats.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $300-700M, overlapping local inference tools market and enterprise on-premise AI

ranked_gaps.title: Mobile/Embedded KG-Reasoning SDK
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: RDFox is the ONLY enterprise option for edge KG, validating demand. Multiple academic papers (EdgeRAG, EACO-RAG, DGRAG) targeting edge. ExecuTorch 1.0 GA proves mobile AI infra is ready.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: RDFox is proprietary and expensive, no SLM integration. MiniRAG is Python-only, no mobile SDK. No iOS/Android native KG+SLM SDK exists.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Harder than server-side tools. Requires native mobile development (Swift/Kotlin), compact graph storage, and integration with on-device inference (CoreML, ExecuTorch). Memory and battery constraints add complexity.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A cross-platform (iOS/Android/embedded) SDK providing compact KG storage + on-device SLM inference in a single library. C++ core with Swift/Kotlin bindings. Targets <50MB total footprint.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Mobile app developers adding offline AI, automotive AI teams, robotics engineers, healthcare field workers, military/defense.
ranked_gaps.why_now: ExecuTorch 1.0 GA (Oct 2025) enables production mobile inference. SLMs at 1-3B now fit in mobile RAM. Edge AI market accelerating.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High — 6-9 months. Cross-platform native code, memory optimization, battery-aware scheduling.

ranked_gaps.title: Domain-Specific KG+SLM Vertical Solutions
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: Galaxy 2026 report confirms enterprise demand for AI-ready semantic layers. Healthcare, finance, and legal verticals have strong compliance drivers for on-premise SLMs + KGs. SLM market projected at $20.7B by 2030.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: All existing tools are general-purpose. Neo4j + custom builds are expensive. BioKGBench is academic only. No turnkey vertical KG+SLM solution.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Requires deep domain expertise per vertical. Building one vertical is feasible (4-6 months), but scaling across verticals is resource-intensive. Domain ontologies and compliance requirements vary significantly.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: Pick ONE vertical (healthcare recommended — strongest demand + compliance drivers). Pre-built medical ontology KG + fine-tuned 3B medical SLM + HIPAA-compliant on-premise deployment. Package as turnkey product.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Healthcare providers needing AI diagnostic support without cloud data exposure, financial compliance teams, legal document analysis firms.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Regulatory pressure (HIPAA, GDPR, EU AI Act) pushing enterprises toward on-premise AI. SLMs now good enough for domain-specific tasks.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High — 6-12 months per vertical. Domain expertise, regulatory compliance, data curation required.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $500M-1B+ across healthcare/finance/legal AI verticals (but requires picking one to start)

ranked_gaps.title: Embedded KG File Format for Offline SLM Reasoning
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: GGUF revolutionized model distribution — no KG equivalent exists. RDFox validates offline KG demand. Air-gapped environments (defense, healthcare) have strong need. 30% of companies struggle with offline AI systems.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: No standardized format exists. SQLite exports and JSON dumps are ad-hoc. Zero direct competition for a purpose-built KG distribution format.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: File format design is technically tractable. Can build on existing serialization (FlatBuffers, Cap'n Proto). Challenge is ecosystem adoption — needs buy-in from KG tool builders.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A binary file format (e.g., .kguf) for packaging knowledge graphs with metadata, optimized for memory-mapped random access. Includes CLI tools for conversion from Neo4j/RDF/JSON exports. Companion library for loading alongside GGUF models.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Offline-first developers, defense/military contractors, healthcare field workers, disaster response teams, researchers distributing KG datasets.
ranked_gaps.why_now: GGUF proved the model — standardization unlocks ecosystem. HuggingFace-style distribution for KGs is the logical next step.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium — 3-4 months for format spec + reference implementation. Adoption is the harder challenge.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $50-150M directly; enables $500M+ in offline AI applications

ranked_gaps.title: End-to-End KG Construction + SLM Fine-Tuning Pipeline
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: Neo4j KG Builder is 4th most popular AuraDB interaction. Practitioners manually stitching 4-5 tools. Active guides comparing models for KG construction. CoDe-KG and AutoBioKG show pipeline demand.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: Neo4j KG Builder does construction but no fine-tuning. EasyDistill does distillation but no KG. LlamaIndex has KG modules but fragmented. No single tool covers documents -> KG -> SLM training -> deployment.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Each stage has mature components. The integration work is substantial but not novel. HuggingFace ecosystem provides training infrastructure.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A Python CLI/library: ingest documents -> auto-build KG -> generate training data from KG -> fine-tune SLM -> export quantized model + KG for deployment. Opinionated defaults, one-command execution.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: ML teams building domain-specific KG-augmented SLMs without DevOps expertise. Solo developers and small teams.
ranked_gaps.why_now: All component tools are mature. The gap is purely in integration and developer experience.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium — 4-5 months. Integration engineering, not research.

ranked_gaps.title: GNN-Enhanced SLM Reasoning Framework
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: GNN-RAG proved 7B models can match GPT-4 on KGQA. 2026 marked as the year GNN+LLM moves to enterprise. Awesome-Graph-LLM repo (1000+ stars) shows developer interest.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: GNN-RAG is research code only. No production-ready framework bridges GNN subgraph reasoning with SLMs. PyG is low-level. The gap between papers and products is wide.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Requires GNN expertise (scarce). Performance tuning GNN+SLM pipelines is non-trivial. PyTorch Geometric is the foundation but needs significant wrapping for production use.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A production-ready Python library wrapping GNN-RAG methodology: given a KG and query, use GNN to retrieve reasoning subgraphs, serialize them for SLM consumption, and return grounded answers. Pre-trained GNN models for common KG schemas.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Developers building knowledge-grounded Q&A systems, enterprise search teams, research labs productionizing KGQA results.
ranked_gaps.why_now: GNN-RAG results are compelling (2025 ACL). GNN+LLM integration moving to enterprise in 2026. First production framework captures the market.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High — 5-7 months. Requires GNN expertise and careful performance optimization.

ranked_gaps.title: KG-Guided Prompt Optimization for SLMs
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: KG-Prompt paper validates the approach. GReaTer shows SLM-specific prompt optimization is active research area. Token budget constraints with small models create real pain.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: KG-Prompt is an academic paper, not a tool. GReaTer is gradient-based but not KG-specific. No production tool converts KG topology into SLM-optimized prompts.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Prompt optimization is well-understood. KG structural analysis (metapaths, subgraphs) is standard. Combining them is novel but technically straightforward.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A prompt engineering library that takes a KG subgraph + query and generates token-budget-optimal prompts for SLMs. Auto-selects between linearization strategies (triples, paths, summaries) based on model size and context window.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Prompt engineers, RAG system builders, developers working with context-limited SLMs (1-3B parameter models with 2-4K context).
ranked_gaps.why_now: SLM context windows are the bottleneck. Efficient KG-to-prompt conversion directly improves answer quality.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Low-Medium — 2-3 months for MVP. Mostly software engineering, limited ML research needed.

ranked_gaps.title: KG-to-SLM Knowledge Distillation Toolkit
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: THINKSLM paper shows CoT doesn't work for SLMs — structural knowledge injection needed. EasyDistill toolkit exists for general distillation but not KG-specific. Academic interest is strong but practitioner awareness is emerging.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: EasyDistill covers general LLM distillation but not KG-to-SLM. KG-MASD is paper-only. Contextualization Distillation targets KG completion, not SLM reasoning. Niche is open but adjacent tools exist.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Harder research problem. KG-to-weight distillation is not as well-understood as standard knowledge distillation. Requires ML research, not just engineering.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A toolkit that takes a KG + base SLM and produces a KG-aware fine-tuned SLM. Supports multiple distillation strategies: embedding injection, curriculum learning from KG paths, contrastive learning on triples.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: ML researchers, teams deploying KG-grounded reasoning on edge devices where runtime KG retrieval is too expensive.
ranked_gaps.why_now: SLM quality improving rapidly. KG distillation is the next frontier after standard knowledge distillation matures.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High — 6-9 months. Active ML research required, not just engineering.

ranked_gaps.title: Open-Source SLM+KG Managed Service
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: Cost-driven SLM adoption is real. KG hosting is the new bottleneck. Users explicitly switching from cloud LLMs to local to save money.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: Neo4j AuraDB and Memgraph Cloud exist but target enterprise LLM use cases. No affordable managed KG+SLM service. However, generic graph DB hosting partially addresses this.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Managed services require significant infrastructure investment, operations expertise, and ongoing maintenance. Capital-intensive to build and scale.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A managed platform: upload documents, auto-build KG, attach SLM, get API endpoint. Pay-per-query pricing. Open-source core with hosted tier.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Startups and indie developers who want KG+SLM without ops burden. Small teams without DevOps capacity.
ranked_gaps.why_now: SLM inference costs are near-zero; KG hosting is the remaining expense. Serverless KG could complete the cost equation.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Very High — 9-12 months. Infrastructure, reliability, billing, support.

ranked_gaps.title: Temporal Knowledge Graph Support for SLMs
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: Graphiti HN thread shows interest in temporal KG for agents. New research on temporal KG distillation. But practitioner awareness is ahead of demand.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: Graphiti requires large LLMs. No temporal KG solution works natively with SLMs. The niche is very open.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Temporal reasoning adds complexity to both KG storage and SLM prompting. Research is still maturing.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A temporal KG layer for SLMs: time-stamped facts, temporal query resolution, and SLM-friendly temporal context serialization.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: IoT/edge applications, financial trading systems, news/event monitoring with local models.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Agent memory increasingly needs temporal awareness. Graphiti proved the concept but locked it behind large LLMs.

ranked_gaps.title: Automated KG Quality Evaluation for SLM Consumption
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: WebQSP only 52% factual correctness highlights KG quality issues. But this is a tooling feature, not a standalone product.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: No tool evaluates KG quality from SLM perspective. Generic graph metrics tools exist but miss SLM-specific properties.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Defining SLM-readiness metrics is tractable. Implementation is straightforward graph analysis.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A KG quality scoring library measuring SLM-relevant properties: average path length, entity density, relationship clarity, context window fit, reasoning chain availability.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Data engineers preparing KGs for SLM integration.
ranked_gaps.why_now: As KG+SLM adoption grows, quality assurance becomes critical. Better as a feature in larger tools.

ranked_gaps.title: Unified Graph DB Adapter for SLM Frameworks
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: MiniRAG's 10+ DB support shows demand exists. But it's an incremental improvement, not a burning pain point.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: MiniRAG already partially addresses this. LlamaIndex has graph store integrations. The gap is shrinking.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Standard adapter pattern. Well-understood engineering problem.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: SLM-aware query planning layer that translates reasoning needs into optimized graph traversals across different DB backends.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams with existing graph infrastructure adding SLM reasoning.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Incremental need. Existing tools are catching up.

ranked_gaps.title: Serverless KG-Augmented SLM Inference
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: Serverless AI trend is real (Modal, Replicate) but KG+SLM serverless is ahead of demand.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: No serverless KG+SLM offering exists. But serverless inference platforms could add this as a feature.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Cold start for both KG loading and model loading is challenging. State management across invocations is complex.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: Serverless endpoint: upload KG + model, get pay-per-query API. Auto-scale to zero.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Very High — 9-12 months. Infrastructure and cold-start optimization.

ranked_gaps.title: Educational/Tutoring KG-SLM Platform
ranked_gaps.demand_rationale: Very limited demand signals. One paper on physics reasoning from educational perspective. Education sector moves slowly.
ranked_gaps.competition_rationale: No competition because no one is building this — which itself may be a signal of insufficient demand.
ranked_gaps.feasibility_rationale: Requires curriculum ontology development, pedagogical expertise, and school IT infrastructure considerations.
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A tutoring platform with curriculum KGs + local SLM reasoning for offline-capable personalized learning.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: EdTech companies, schools in low-connectivity areas.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Not urgent. Long sales cycles in education.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High — 6-9 months. Domain expertise + EdTech distribution challenges.
